brokerCatalog.name: FastPeopleSearch
brokerCatalog.plainDescription: Opt-out-only broker. The public search path reliably falls into rate-limit and survey upsell flows, so Infobreaker skips discovery and queues the removal flow directly.
brokerCatalog.userNote: Search is off because the free search path is a rate-limit/survey upsell funnel. Infobreaker includes FastPeopleSearch in removals automatically.

brokerPlaybook.suppressionLane: Run the FastPeopleSearch removal flow directly. Do not require scan evidence first.
knownQuirks: Manual tests across multiple browsers and systems land on /rate-limited, then a survey/email-capture upsell instead of a useful search result surface.
Treat search failures here as expected site behavior, not an Infobreaker module breakage.
The removal flow is still useful and requires email confirmation plus CAPTCHA-assisted browser steps.

trustMetadata.evidenceReason: Infobreaker can assist or submit a request, but broker-side completion still needs follow-up evidence.

brokerCatalog.name: Whitepages
brokerCatalog.plainDescription: Large people-search directory. Scans are useful, but removal can require extra verification.

deletionLane.notes: Email Whitepages for full deletion. This route avoids the phone call in its form.

brokerPlaybook.emailFallback: Use the broker-cited privacy request address when the phone-code suppression tool is impractical.
knownQuirks: The automated suppression tool can require a phone callback.
Broker confirmation is not proof of removal; scheduled verification still matters.

optOutPlaybook: Save the listing link before you start.
Use the privacy-request email lane when a phone callback is not practical.
Review the request details before sending them to the broker.
Save the broker reply. Then plan a new scan.
quirks: The standard suppression lane may ask for a phone callback.
A broker acknowledgement is not proof that the listing is gone.

brokerCatalog.name: Intelius
brokerCatalog.impactReason: PeopleConnect suppression can affect multiple affiliated properties.
brokerCatalog.plainDescription: PeopleConnect broker family. One workflow can matter across multiple affiliated sites.
brokerCatalog.userNote: PeopleConnect family

brokerPlaybook.suppressionLane: Suppress and maintain the PeopleConnect record; this is the lane that keeps affiliated people-search sites from showing the listing.

deletionLane.notes: Use suppression to stay out of search results. Deletion can clear suppression and let public records return.

brokerPlaybook.emailFallback: Reserve the privacy email for a deliberate rights request or broken portal follow-up, not as a replacement for maintained suppression.
knownQuirks: Work this parent before child brands, then re-scan children.
Child-brand search forms can submit into Intelius/PeopleConnect tracking flows; do not treat those controls as standalone removal coverage.
TruthFinder/PubRec-affiliate surfaces often lead to paid-report funnels; keep them as parent-family guidance unless a broker-owned result surface is fixture-covered.
Several child brands route privacy links through Intelius privacy/suppression pages, so the parent suppression outcome matters more than each child form.
The verification link is session-bound; open it in the same browser session that requested it.
A completed PeopleConnect deletion email means suppression may have been removed and should be re-run.

urlFormatQuirks: Enter date of birth as YYYY-MM-DD when the form asks for it.
optOutPlaybook: Start with the PeopleConnect privacy flow before working on child brands.
Check the details. Then submit the form.
Keep the same assisted browser open while you wait for the check link.
Open the check link promptly; the expected window is about 15 minutes.
Treat the result as suppression, then re-scan each covered site.
quirks: The check link may be tied to the browser session that requested it.
Suppression hides a listing but does not prove that stored data was deleted.

brokerCatalog.name: Spokeo
brokerCatalog.impactReason: Major people-search brand and broad exposure surface.
brokerCatalog.plainDescription: Major people-search broker. Valuable coverage, but the site can push users toward paid-report funnels.
brokerCatalog.userNote: Scans are off because Spokeo search results sit behind a paid-report funnel. Removals still run through the Spokeo opt-out page.

deletionLane.notes: First hide each listing. Then email Spokeo to ask for full deletion.

brokerPlaybook.emailFallback: Use the privacy address for full rights-request follow-up or when the form rejects a confirmed listing.
knownQuirks: Multiple listing URLs may exist for one person; each URL can need its own opt-out.
Free-search suppression is not the same as full data deletion.

optOutPlaybook: Search each saved name and place before you start.
Collect each distinct public listing link that belongs to the profile.
Submit one opt-out request for each listing link.
Finish each email check. Then scan all names and places again.
quirks: A single request may cover only one listing link.
Different search variations can expose more than one listing link.

trustMetadata.evidenceReason: Infobreaker can assist or submit a request, but broker-side completion still needs follow-up evidence.

brokerCatalog.name: BeenVerified
brokerCatalog.impactReason: Large LTVCo broker network with multiple affiliated products.
brokerCatalog.plainDescription: Large broker network with high value when a correct match is found. Requires careful match review.
brokerCatalog.userNote: Paywalled results can limit what verification can prove.

deletionLane.notes: Hide the listing. This is step one. Then email the broker. Ask it to delete your data from: BeenVerified, NeighborWho, Ownerly, NumberGuru, and Bumper.

brokerPlaybook.emailFallback: Use the privacy address for rights-request follow-up across LTVCo/BeenVerified properties after matching is reviewed.
knownQuirks: Paywalled result surfaces can limit verification strength.
FreePhoneTracer and NumberGuru are phone-lookup surfaces; do not scan them from a name-only workflow.
Ownerly and NeighborWho are property/address-heavy surfaces; treat them as Extended Records unless an address workflow is explicitly selected.
Do not assume BeenVerified opt-out clears every LTVCo sister property without follow-up verification.

optOutPlaybook: Check the parent service and its covered sister properties.
Send one request for each email check.
Finish that email check before you start the next request.
Re-scan each covered property after the broker processes the request.
quirks: One email-confirmation flow may handle only one opt-out request.
Later scans must check each sister site.

brokerCatalog.name: CourtRecords.us
brokerCatalog.plainDescription: CourtRecords.us state-site network. Use as Extended Records: it may show public-record exposure, but the broker is a preview/paywall funnel and removal is an assisted privacy request.
brokerCatalog.userNote: Extended Records source: removal here is record-by-record, manual, or unavailable — not a standard people-search opt-out.

brokerPlaybook.suppressionLane: Use the state-site opt-out/search form or CourtRecords.us privacy form; treat as assisted until a later re-scan verifies removal.
brokerPlaybook.emailFallback: Use the privacy address for rights-request follow-up when the state-site removal form is blocked or incomplete.
knownQuirks: The public scan is a preliminary teaser/paywall surface, not a full court-record report.
State sites share the same form and members.courtrecords.us removal backend.
Do not turn a form submission into a removal claim until verification confirms the listing is gone.

brokerCatalog.name: State Arrests
brokerCatalog.plainDescription: State Arrests / InfoTracer affiliate network. Use as Extended Records: the state sites expose public-record search forms, but searches route into an InfoTracer affiliate/loading funnel and removal is an assisted InfoTracer privacy request.
brokerCatalog.userNote: Extended Records source: removal here is record-by-record, manual, or unavailable — not a standard people-search opt-out.

brokerPlaybook.suppressionLane: Use InfoTracer opt-out for record removal requests, then re-scan the originating state site later. Treat completion as assisted until verified.
brokerPlaybook.emailFallback: Use the InfoTracer privacy flow first; add a rights-request fallback only after a primary-source review identifies a current address.
knownQuirks: State-site forms route into an infotracer.net loading/affiliate funnel rather than a trustworthy local result surface.
Some state domains use abbreviations, such as flarrests.org, ctarrests.org, and nmarrests.org.
Do not turn a form submission or loading page into a public-listing match until a verified result surface is observed.

brokerCatalog.userNote: Extended Records source: removal here is record-by-record, manual, or unavailable — not a standard people-search opt-out.

brokerPlaybook.suppressionLane: Use the state-site opt-out page for the specific listing, then re-scan the originating site later. Treat as assisted until verified.
brokerPlaybook.emailFallback: Use the site opt-out form first; add a rights-request fallback only after a primary-source review identifies a current address.
knownQuirks: State sites share q[full_name] search forms, but some duplicate hidden forms appear before the visible form.
Coverage is state-limited. Unsupported states return no match rather than falling through to a wrong state directory.
Resident/voter directory exposure is Extended Records, not a normal default people-search opt-out lane.

brokerCatalog.name: ThePublicIndex
brokerCatalog.plainDescription: ThePublicIndex / InfoPay public-records index. Use as Extended Records: the homepage/state pages expose public-record search controls, but no trustworthy people-result surface was observed; removal is an assisted privacy form.
brokerCatalog.userNote: Extended Records source: removal here is record-by-record, manual, or unavailable — not a standard people-search opt-out.

brokerPlaybook.suppressionLane: Use the ThePublicIndex privacy choices form at /optout. Treat as assisted until a later re-scan verifies the listing is gone.
brokerPlaybook.emailFallback: Use the privacy address for covered-person requests or when the removal form is blocked or incomplete.
knownQuirks: The homepage and state pages expose name/state search controls, but no trustworthy public people-result surface was observed in no-PII browser review.
The opt-out form posts to members.thepublicindex.org/removeMyData/ and asks for first name, last name, state, and optional city.
State subdomains are public-record explainer/index pages, not proof that a person listing exists.
Do not turn a form submission into a removal claim until verification confirms the listing is gone.

brokerCatalog.name: Unmask
brokerCatalog.plainDescription: Affiliate report funnel. Current public search redirects to information.com instead of exposing a useful Unmask result surface.
brokerCatalog.userNote: Scans are off because the site redirects to a paid-report funnel, so scan results prove nothing. Removal uses the direct opt-out.

trustMetadata.evidenceReason: This broker is tracked for manual, monitoring, or discovery-only use; outcome proof is limited.

brokerCatalog.name: Classmates
brokerCatalog.plainDescription: Yearbook and school directory (PeopleConnect family). Shows identity exposure beyond addresses; not a normal listing removal.
brokerCatalog.userNote: Extended Records source: removal here is record-by-record, manual, or unavailable — not a standard people-search opt-out.

brokerPlaybook.suppressionLane: Use the PeopleConnect suppression portal to keep this result hidden.

deletionLane.notes: Use suppression to stay out of search results. Deletion can clear suppression and let public records return.

knownQuirks: Do not cancel or delete an account as a way to hide a people-search result.

trustMetadata.evidenceReason: This broker is tracked for manual, monitoring, or discovery-only use; outcome proof is limited.

brokerCatalog.name: Acxiom
brokerCatalog.impactReason: Wholesale identity/data broker with downstream ecosystem impact.
brokerCatalog.plainDescription: Wholesale data broker. No public profile search exists, but opt-out can reduce downstream re-seeding risk.
brokerCatalog.userNote: Acxiom has no public profile page to prove found or removed. The app submits one suppression request, stores broker confirmation evidence, then waits at least 90 days before allowing another request.

brokerPlaybook.suppressionLane: Request broker-level suppression even though there is no public profile page to scan.
knownQuirks: Completion is broker-confirmation evidence plus downstream monitoring, not direct listing verification.

trustMetadata.evidenceReason: Infobreaker can assist or submit a request, but broker-side completion still needs follow-up evidence.
